content.problemStatement:
Chef is baking a large cake! The cake consists of NNN different ingredients numbered 111 through NNN. Chef can choose how much of each ingredient to use in his cake recipe; let's denote the amount of the iii-th ingredient used in the cake by gig_igi​. Chef can only measure ingredients in fixed whole units, so the amounts of all ingredients must be integers.

In order to keep the cake inexpensive (and, of course, use each ingredient from the recipe), the chosen amounts of ingredients have to satisfy the condition 1≤gi≤K1 \le g_i \le K1≤gi​≤K (for each 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N). The taste of the resulting cake is computed as the bitwise XOR of the amounts of all ingredients, i.e. g1⊕g2⊕⋯⊕gNg_1 \oplus g_2 \oplus \dots \oplus g_Ng1​⊕g2​⊕⋯⊕gN​. For example, if N=3N=3N=3 and g=[1,2,5]g = [1, 2, 5]g=[1,2,5], the taste of the cake is equal to 1⊕2⊕5=61 \oplus 2 \oplus 5 = 61⊕2⊕5=6.

Please help Chef and find one way to choose the amounts of all ingredients such that the taste of the cake is maximum possible.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing N space-separated integers g1, g2, …, gN — the amounts of ingredients 1, 2, …, N.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the maximum possible taste is 5 ⊕ 1 ⊕ 3 = 7. For the second test case, the maximum possible taste is 1 ⊕ 2 ⊕ 3 ⊕ 3 = 3.

solution.approach: To maximize the taste of the cake, we need to maximize the bitwise XOR of the amounts of all ingredients. We can achieve this by setting the amount of each ingredient to K if N is odd, and to K if the index of the ingredient is odd and to 1 if the index is even if N is even. This way, we ensure that the bitwise XOR of the amounts is maximized.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — we loop over each test case and for each test case, we loop over each ingredient
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — we need to store the amounts of all ingredients
